While the F-47 contract win and duopoly position provide long-term optionality, the combination of FAA production caps, a criminal guilty plea, deteriorating margins, and near-junk credit ratings make the risk/reward unattractive over a one-year horizon.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Boeing is technically insolvent with negative equity of -$3.91B, TTM net losses of -$11.83B, and negative gross margins across the trailing year. While the F-47 contract win and duopoly position provide long-term optionality, the combination of FAA production caps, a criminal guilty plea, deteriorating margins, and near-junk credit ratings make the risk/reward unattractive over a one-year horizon.

Technically insolvent with negative equity of -$3.91B (Q4 2024) and $53.62B in debt; credit rating near junk status per Reuters (2025-02-10), limiting financial flexibility.
FAA production rate cap at 38/month for 737 MAX (10-K, 2025-02-03) directly constrains revenue ramp in Boeing's largest commercial segment, with no timeline for removal.

Criminal guilty plea for fraud related to 737 MAX crashes (2025-02-28), active whistleblower litigation (2025-03-19/21), and ongoing FAA oversight create sustained legal and regulatory overhang that could impair capital allocation.

e narrowly focused entities. Across our business segments, we recognize hundreds of competitors worldwide and as we execute our hybrid cloud and AI strategy, we are regularly exposed to new competitors.
Our principal methods of competition are: technology innovation; performance; price; quality; brand; our breadth of capabilities, products and services; talent; client relationships and trust; the ability to deliver business value to clients; and service and support. In order to maintain leadership, we optimize our portfolio with organic and inorganic innovations and effective resource allocation. These investments not only drive current performance but will extend our innovation leadership into the future.
Our breadth and depth of expertise enables us to take different technologies and bring them together to solve the most pressing business issues of our clients. We differentiate from other providers by bringing together incumbency with enterprises, deep expertise in technology, industries and business processes, a broad go-to-market reach including an ecosystem of partners and alliances, and a strong focus on innovation. All of these attributes position IBM for accelerated growth now and prepare us for the next set of business opportunities, such as quantum computing.
1
Table of Contents
Overall, the company is the leader or among the leaders in each of our business segments. A summary of the competitive environment for each business segment is included below:
Software:
The depth, breadth, and innovation of our software offerings, coupled with our global reach, deep industry expertise and research capabilities help differentiate our offerings from our competitors. Our hybrid cloud and AI platforms allow clients to realize their digital and AI transformations across the applications, data, and environments in which they operate. The principal competitors in this segment include: Alphabet (Google), Amazon, BMC, Broadcom, Informatica, Microsoft, Oracle, Salesforce, SAP and Splunk. We also compete with smaller, niche competitors in specific geographic regions or product segments.
Consulting:
Consulting focuses on integrating skills on strategy, experience, technology and operations by domain and industry. Consulting competes in a dynamic market including consulting, systems integration, application development, application management and business process outsourcing services. Our broad-based competitors include: Accenture, Capgemini, India-based service providers, management consulting firms, the consulting practices of public accounting firms, engineering service providers, and many companies that primarily focus on local markets or niche service areas.
Infrastructure:
IBM is well positioned in the growing hybrid cloud infrastructure market, providing on-premises and cloud-based server and storage solutions. We gain advantage and differentiation through investments in higher-value capabilities, including security, scalability, and reliability, designed especially for mission-critical and AI workloads. In addition, we offer a portfolio of life-cycle services for hybrid cloud infrastructure deployment. Our principal competitors include: Dell Technologies, Hewlett-Packard Enterprise (HPE), Intel, NetApp and Pure Storage as well as original device manufacturers (ODMs) who provide systems that are re-branded. Further, cloud service providers are leveraging innovation in technology and service delivery to compete with traditional providers and to offer additional routes to market for server and storage systems.
Financing:
Financing provides client and commercial financing, facilitating IBM clients' acquisition of hardware, software and services. Financing's ability to manage credit and residual value risk generates a competitive advantage for the company. The key competitive factors include: interest rates charged, IT product experience, client service, contract flexibility, ease of doing business, global capabilities and residual values. In client and commercial financing, Financing primarily competes with non-captive financing entities and financial institutions.

In addition, the company's representatives may from time to time make oral forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements provide current expectations of future events based on certain assumptions and include any statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact. Words such as "anticipates," "believes," "expects," "estimates," "intends," "plans," "projects," and similar expressions, may identify such forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statement in this Form 10-K speaks only as of the date on which it is made. Except as required by law, the company assumes no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements. In accordance with the Reform Act, set forth under Item 1A. "Risk Factors" on pages 3 to 9 are cautionary statements that accompany those forward-looking statements. Readers should carefully review such cautionary statements as they identify certain important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those in the forward-looking statements and from historical trends. Those cautionary statements are not exclusive and are in addition to other factors discussed elsewhere in this Form 10-K, in the company's filings with the SEC or in materials incorporated therein by reference.

IBM.quantitative_report:
# IBM — Quantitative Report (Decision Date: 2025-04-01)

## 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue:** TTM revenue of $62.75B. Q4 2024 revenue of $17.55B grew only +1.0% YoY versus Q4 2023's $17.38B. Revenue is highly seasonal, with Q4 consistently the largest quarter. Sequential Q3→Q4 jump was +17.3% ($14.97B→$17.55B), but this pattern repeats annually and does not signal acceleration.

**Earnings:** TTM net income of $6.02B. Q4 2024 EPS of $3.11 declined -12.6% YoY from Q4 2023's $3.56. Critically, Q3 2024 posted a **net loss** of -$330M (EPS = -$0.36), with operating income of -$802M. The preceding 4-quarter EPS sequence (newest first): $3.11, -$0.36, $1.96, $1.72.

**Margins:**
| Period | Gross Margin | Operating Margin | Net Margin |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q4 2024 | 59.5% (10.44/17.55) | 18.9% (3.31/17.55) | 16.6% (2.91/17.55) |
| Q3 2024 | 56.2% (8.42/14.97) | -5.4% (loss) | Negative |
| Q4 2023 | 59.1% (10.27/17.38) | 21.6% (3.76/17.38) | 18.9% (3.29/17.38) |

TTM operating margin: 9.2% ($5.80B / $62.75B). TTM net margin: 9.6%. Both well below Q4 2023 trailing levels (~13% net margin implied by the valuation panel's 2024 mid-year readings). **Gross margin is stable (~56-60%), but operating and net margins have compressed materially**, partly driven by the deeply negative Q3 2024 quarter.

**Cash Flow:** **Completely unavailable.** All quarterly cash-flow entries are "n/a" and TTM free cash flow is "n/a." This is a significant evidentiary gap.

**Balance Sheet:** Total equity grew from $22.61B (Q4 2023) to $27.39B (Q4 2024), a +21.2% increase. Total liabilities decreased from $112.63B to $109.78B (-2.5%). Total assets grew modestly from $135.24B to $137.18B (+1.4%).

---

## 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings Quality — Concerns:**
- The Q3 2024 loss (net income -$330M, operating income -$802M) is an outlier amid otherwise positive quarters. This raises questions about the stability and predictability of earnings.
- Quarterly EPS volatility is extreme: from -$0.36 (Q3 2024) to $3.11 (Q4 2024) in consecutive quarters.
- YoY Q4 EPS declined -12.6% despite revenue growing +1.0%, indicating margin erosion.
- Gross margins are stable (~59% in Q4), but operating costs consumed a larger share. TTM operating income of $5.80B on $62.75B revenue yields a thin 9.2% operating margin.
- **Cash flow data is entirely absent**, so cash conversion cannot be verified. No assessment of accruals quality or free-cash-flow-to-net-income alignment is possible.

**Balance-Sheet Resilience — Mixed:**
- Equity-to-assets: 27.39 / 137.18 = **20.0%** — thin for a $137B-asset company.
- Liabilities-to-equity: 109.78 / 27.39 = **4.01x** — elevated leverage.
- Equity improved YoY by $4.78B, which is a positive trend.
- However, with $109.78B in liabilities against only $5.80B in TTM operating income, the liability coverage is thin. The absence of cash-flow data prevents assessment of debt serviceability.

---

## 3. Valuation Versus Own Supplied History

| Metric | Current (2025-03-31) | Window Low | Window High | Position |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| P/E diluted | **38.672** | 18.8 | 40.7 | **96th percentile** |
| P/B | **8.507** | 6.6 | 10.0 | 56th percentile |
| P/S | **3.714** | n/a | n/a | — |
| Earnings yield | **2.6%** | n/a | n/a | — |
| Net margin | **9.6%** | ~10% | ~13.5% | **Below range** |
| ROE | **22.0%** | ~20% | ~35% | **Bottom of range** |

**Key observation:** The P/E has nearly **doubled** from 18.8x (May 2024) to 38.7x (March 2025) while net margin **contracted** from 13.2% to 9.6% and ROE **collapsed** from 35.1% to 22.0%. The market is paying a near-record multiple for meaningfully deteriorating profitability metrics. This is pure multiple expansion disconnected from improving fundamentals.

At a P/E of 38.7x and an earnings yield of 2.6%, IBM is priced for sustained growth that the TTM trajectory (+1.0% revenue growth, -12.6% YoY EPS decline) does not support.

---

## 4. Price Level and Return Context

**Price:** $248.66 (2025-03-31). 52-week range: $162.62–$266.45. Current price is at **86% of the high** and **53% above the low**.

| Period | Starting Price | Return |
|---|---|---|
| Latest quarter (~63d) | $224.89 (Dec 26) | **+10.6%** |
| Prior quarter | $223.43 (Sep 26) | +0.7% |
| Q3 2024 | $170.85 (Jun 27) | +30.8% |
| Q2 2024 | $190.80 (Mar 27) | -10.5% |
| **Annual (~252d)** | $190.80 (Mar 27) | **+30.3%** |

The stock has returned **+30.3% over the past year** while TTM EPS declined and margins compressed. The +30.8% quarterly surge in Q3 2024 occurred alongside the Q3 loss quarter — a clear disconnect. Price momentum has been driven entirely by multiple expansion (P/E from ~19x to ~39x), not by fundamental improvement.

---

## 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside

**Upside Case:**
- If net margins revert toward the 13.2% level observed in mid-2024 on the current TTM revenue base of $62.75B, net income would be approximately $8.28B (vs. current TTM of $6.02B), implying EPS recovery of ~37%.
- If the current 38.7x P/E is sustained on normalized earnings, price appreciation of ~37% from earnings recovery alone is mathematically possible.
- Equity growth (+21.2% YoY) could support balance-sheet deleveraging.

**Downside Case:**
- P/E mean-reversion to the midpoint of its supplied range (~29.8x) on current TTM EPS of ~$6.50/share (~$6.02B / ~926M diluted shares implied by Q4 EPS math) would imply a price near **~$194**, representing **-22% downside** from $248.66.
- If P/E reverts to the low end of the range (18.8x) on unchanged earnings, the implied price is ~$122, or **-51% downside**.
- The Q3 2024 loss quarter raises the risk of recurring negative quarters, which would further compress the multiple.
- With liabilities-to-equity at 4.01x, any deterioration in operating performance amplifies balance-sheet stress.

---

## 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

The quantitative evidence is unfavorable. IBM trades at a **near-record P/E of 38.7x** (96th percentile of its supplied range) while simultaneously exhibiting:

- **Declining profitability:** net margin 13.2% → 9.6%, ROE 35.1% → 22.0% over the trailing window.
- **Stagnant growth:** +1.0% YoY revenue growth, -12.6% YoY EPS decline in Q4.
- **Earnings instability:** a Q3 2024 net loss of -$330M amid a volatile quarterly EPS pattern.
- **High leverage:** 4.01x liabilities-to-equity, 20% equity ratio.
- **Zero cash-flow visibility:** all cash-flow data unavailable, preventing quality validation.

The +30.3% annual price return is entirely explained by P/E expansion (from ~19x to ~39x), not by fundamental improvement. This creates asymmetric downside: any multiple normalization toward historical norms would compound with already-weak fundamentals, while upside requires both margin recovery **and** sustained elevated valuation — a double assumption. The risk/reward profile is skewed negative on the supplied point-in-time evidence.
IBM.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Report: IBM (as of 2025-04-01)

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 2025-02-25 10-K)**
IBM is a globally integrated technology company focused on the intersection of hybrid cloud and artificial intelligence (AI). Its strategy is to help clients leverage these technologies for digital transformation. The business comprises four segments: **Software** (hybrid cloud and AI platforms), **Consulting** (integrating strategy, technology, and operations), **Infrastructure** (hybrid cloud server and storage solutions), and **Financing**. The company differentiates itself through its breadth of capabilities (software, consulting, infrastructure), deep industry expertise, established client relationships, and a strong R&D organization. It proactively partners with major hyperscalers (AWS, Microsoft) and other tech leaders. IBM identifies competition as intense and global across all segments, with numerous large and niche players.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated Evidence)**
*   **Guidance & Outlook (2025-03-03, 2025-02-10, 2025-03-02):** Management has provided bullish forward guidance. As of late February/March 2025, IBM expects 2025 revenue growth of at least 5% (constant currency) and adjusted EBITDA growth in the double digits. Management raised its growth targets.
*   **Market Position & Performance (2025-03-10, 2025-03-20):** The stock was noted to have delivered a ~19% gain in early 2025, outpacing the broader market. It was highlighted as a top stock to buy for its strong financial position and growth prospects.
*   **Strategic Partnerships & Technology (2025-03-19):** NVIDIA announced a collaboration with IBM (among others) for its Accelerated Quantum Research Center in Boston, aiming to drive advancements in quantum computing.
*   **Segment-Specific Expectations (2025-02-12):** Analysts anticipated a recovery in the Consulting segment in the second half of 2025.
*   **Corporate Action - DEI Policy (2025-03-13):** IBM removed diversity hiring metrics from executive pay calculations and erased diversity language from its annual report, citing legal challenges and pressure.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Intellectual Property Litigation (2025-03-10):** A UK High Court ruled that a subsidiary of LzLabs reverse-engineered IBM software for a mainframe modernization product. This poses a potential existential threat to LzLabs' business and is a cautionary case for IT buyers, reflecting ongoing IP defense risks.
*   **Risk: Employment Litigation (2025-02-20):** IBM is facing lawsuits alleging it illegally classified thousands of U.S. employees as exempt from overtime. The company responded with a 15% salary cut for some executives. This indicates potential legal and reputational exposure.
*   **Risk: Competitive Pressure:** The 10-K explicitly acknowledges a highly competitive environment with hundreds of competitors, including large hyperscalers, in all business segments.
*   **Offset: Legal Victory (2025-03-10):** The U.S. Supreme Court upheld an IBM win in a $1.6 billion software contract dispute with BMC, supporting IBM's position on contract terms.
*   **Offset: Execution in Growth Areas:** Management guidance and analyst commentary point to successful execution in hybrid cloud and AI, with consulting recovery expected. Partnerships (e.g., with NVIDIA on quantum) reinforce a focus on next-generation technology leadership.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Cautiously Positive.** The confirmed evidence supports a constructive view. IBM's franchise is well-defined around durable secular themes (hybrid cloud, AI). Recent management guidance is explicitly optimistic, and market performance reflects growing confidence. Strategic moves like the quantum collaboration show forward positioning. However, this positivity is tempered by clear, ongoing legal risks (IP and employment litigation) and the inherent challenge of competing in hyper-competitive markets against both established players and agile specialists. The corporate action on DEI policies introduces potential reputational sensitivity. The balance of catalysts (growth guidance, tech partnerships) versus risks (lawsuits, competition) skews the qualitative outlook to positive, but with material caveats.

The +30% annual price return is entirely driven by multiple expansion, not earnings growth, creating asymmetric downside risk if valuations normalize.

Item 1. Business
General
Cisco designs and sells a broad range of technologies that help to power, secure, and draw insights from the Internet. We are integrating artificial intelligence (AI) into our product portfolios across networking, security, collaboration and observability to simplify how our technology is delivered, managed and optimized and to help customers maximize the business value of their technology investments and accelerate their digital transformation.
We conduct our business globally and manage our business by geography. Our business is organized into the following three geographic segments: Americas; Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA); and Asia Pacific, Japan, and China (APJC).
Our products and technologies are grouped into the following categories: Networking, Security, Collaboration and Observability. In addition to our product offerings, we provide a broad ran
…[truncated 21118 chars]…
obtaining business, particularly for those customers involved in significant infrastructure projects. Our financing arrangements include loans, leases (sales-type, direct financing and operating) and channel financing arrangements.
Acquisitions, Investments, and Alliances
The markets in which we compete require a wide variety of technologies, products, and capabilities. We continue to evaluate opportunities to acquire and invest in businesses and technologies that complement and enable further investment in our key priority areas.
Acquisitions
We acquire companies in order to gain access to talent, technology, products and features, operational capabilities or new markets. The risks associated with acquisitions are more fully discussed in "Item 1A. Risk Factors," including the risk factor entitled "We have made and expect to continue to make acquisitions that could disrupt our operations and harm our operating results."
Investments in Privately Held Companies
We make investments in privately held companies that develop technology or provide services that are complementary to our products or that provide insights into emerging technologies that may become relevant to our businesses. The risks associated with these investments are more fully discussed in "Item 1A. Risk Factors," including the risk factor entitled "We are exposed to fluctuations in the market values of our portfolio investments and in interest rates; impairment of our investments could harm our earnings."
5
Table of Contents
Strategic Alliances
We pursue strategic alliances with other companies in areas where collaboration can produce industry advancement and accelerate new markets. The objectives and goals of a strategic alliance can include one or more of the following: technology exchange, product development, joint sales and marketing, or new market creation.
The risks associated with our strategic alliances are more fully discussed in "Item 1A. Risk Factors," including the risk factor entitled "If we do not successfully manage our strategic alliances, we may not realize the expected benefits from such alliances and we may experience increased competition or delays in product development."
Competition
We compete in the networking and communications equipment markets, providing products and services designed to transport, and help secure data, voice, and video traffic across cloud, private and public networks and the Internet. We compete with numerous vendors in each product category. The overall number of our competitors providing niche product solutions may increase. Also, the identity and composition of competitors may change as we increase our activity in newer product areas, and in key priority areas. As we continue to expand globally, we may see new competition in different geographic regions. In particular, we have experienced price-focused competition from competitors in Asia, especially from China, and we anticipate this will continue.
Our competitors (in each case relative to only some of our products or services) include: Amazon Web Services LLC; Arista Networks, Inc.; Broadcom Inc.; Ciena Corporation; CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.; Datadog Inc.; Dell Technologies Inc.; Dynatrace Inc.; Fortinet, Inc.; Hewlett-Packard Enterprise Company; Huawei Technologies Co., Ltd.; Juniper Networks, Inc.; Microsoft Corporation; New Relic, Inc.; Nokia Corporation; Nvidia Corporation; Palo Alto Networks, Inc.; RingCentral, Inc.; Zoom Video Communications, Inc.; and Zscaler, Inc.; among others.
Some of our competitors compete across many of our product lines, while others are primarily focused in a specific product area. Barriers to entry are relatively low, and new ventures to create products that do or could compete with our products are regularly formed. In addition, some of our competitors may have greater resources, including technical and engineering resources, than we do. As we expand into new markets, we will face competition not only from our existing competitors but also from other competitors, including existing companies with strong technological, marketing, and sales positions in those markets. We also sometimes face competition from resellers and distributors of our products. Companies with which we have strategic alliances in some areas may be competitors in other areas, and this trend may increase. For example, the enterprise data center is undergoing a fundamental transformation arising from the convergence of technologies, including computing, networking, storage, and software, that previously were segregated. Additionally, companies that are strategic alliance partners in some areas of our business may acquire or form alliances with our competitors, thereby reducing their business with us.
The principal competitive factors in the markets in which we presently compete and may compete in the future include the ability to sell successful business outcomes; the ability to provide a broad range of networking and communications products and services; product performance; price; the ability to introduce new products, including providing continuous new customer value and products with price-performance advantages; the ability to reduce production costs; the ability to provide value-added features such as security, reliability, and investment protection; conformance to standards; market presence; the ability to provide financing; and disruptive technology shifts and new business models.
We also face competition from customers to which we license or supply technology and suppliers from which we transfer technology. The inherent nature of networking requires interoperability. As such, we must cooperate and, at the same time, compete with many companies. Any inability to effectively manage these complicated relationships with customers, suppliers, and strategic alliance partners could materially harm our business, operating results, and financial condition and accordingly affect our chances of success.
Research and Development
We regularly introduce new products and features to address the requirements of our markets. We allocate our research and development budget among our product categories, which consist of Networking, Security, Collaboration, and Observability technologies. Our research and development expenditures are applied generally to all product areas, with specific areas of focus being identified from time to time. Our expenditures for research and development costs were expensed as incurred.
The industry in which we compete is subject to rapid technological developments, evolving standards, changes in customer requirements, and new product introductions and enhancements. As a result, our success depends, in part, on our ability, on a cost-effective and timely basis, to continue to enhance our existing products and to develop and introduce new products that improve performance and reduce total cost of ownership. To achieve these objectives, our management and engineering personnel work with customers to identify and respond to customer needs, as well as with other innovators of internet networking products, including universities, laboratories, and corporations. We also expect to continue to make acquisitions and

## CSCO Quantitative Report — Decision Date 2025-04-01

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue** bottomed at $12.70B (Q3 FY24, 2024-04-27) after declining from a peak of $15.20B (Q4 FY23, 2023-07-29). The four most recent quarters show sequential recovery: $12.70B → $13.64B → $13.84B → $13.99B. YoY revenue growth in the latest quarter is +9.4%; QoQ growth is a modest +1.1%. TTM revenue is $54.18B.

**Earnings** have not recovered commensurately. EPS peaked at $0.96 (Q4 FY23) and fell to a trough of $0.46 (Q3 FY24). The latest quarter EPS is $0.61, still –36% below the peak. YoY EPS is –6.2%; QoQ EPS is –10.3%. TTM net income is $9.19B.

**Margins:** Gross margin is stable at 65.1% in the latest quarter versus 64.1% a year prior; gross profit grew +10.9% YoY. However, operating income tells a different story: it fell from $4.25–$4.28B per quarter (Q3–Q4 FY23) to $2.19–$3.11B in the latest four quarters (TTM operating income: $10.28B). The supplied operating margin is 19% and net margin is 17%, both at the low end of their respective trailing ranges (operating margin range: 19–19%; net margin range: 17–23.5%). The margin compression sits below the gross-profit line, consistent with elevated operating expenses—likely acquisition-related.

**Cash flow:** No quarterly or annual cash-flow data is supplied; free-cash-flow yield and cash conversion cannot be assessed.

**Balance sheet:** Total debt tripled from $7.70–$8.40B (pre-April 2024) to $20.10B (from 2024-04 onward), while total assets rose from ~$97–101B to ~$121–124B. This step-change coincides with an acquisition (Splunk, per public knowledge but not sourced from supplied numbers). Equity has been stable at $42.3B–$46.3B across all eight quarters. Debt-to-equity is 0.441.

### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality:** Revenue is growing (+9.4% YoY), but earnings are declining (–6.2% YoY EPS, –7.8% YoY net income). Gross-profit growth of +10.9% YoY outpaces revenue growth, indicating improving product/subscription mix, yet net income fell from $2.63B (year-ago quarter) to $2.43B. This gap implies significant cost absorption—likely amortisation, integration, or elevated R&D/SG&A from the acquisition. Net margin has compressed from 23.5% (April 2024) to 17.0% (latest). ROE has fallen from 29.1% to 20.2% over the same window. No cash-flow data is available to test accrual quality or free-cash-flow conversion.

**Balance-sheet resilience:** D/E of 0.441 is manageable but represents a structural step-up versus the prior ~0.17–0.19 level (based on $8.4B debt / $42–45B equity). Total equity of $45.53B comfortably exceeds total debt of $20.10B. However, the absence of cash-flow data limits assessment of debt-service capacity and liquidity headroom.

### 3. Valuation versus Supplied History

| Metric | Latest (2025-03-31) | Window Range | 1-Year-Ago Level (2024-04-01) |
|---|---|---|---|
| P/E diluted | **26.9x** | 14.2–28.3x | 15.2x |
| P/B | **5.43x** | 4.0–5.7x | 4.41x |
| P/S | **4.56x** | n/a | n/a |
| Earnings yield | **3.7%** | n/a | n/a |
| Net margin | 17.0% | 17–23.5% | 23.5% |
| ROE | 20.2% | 20.2–29.1% | 29.1% |

The P/E has expanded nearly 77% (from 15.2x to 26.9x) over the trailing year while EPS has declined and net margin has contracted from 23.5% to 17.0%. The stock now sits at the 95th percentile of its 1-year P/E distribution and near the top of its P/B range (5.43x vs. ceiling of 5.7x). The earnings yield of 3.7% is thin relative to prevailing risk-free rates and offers no margin of safety given the earnings contraction underway. On a pure multiple basis, the valuation is stretched relative to the company's own recent history; however, it is not cheap even on an absolute basis—no fundamental driver supports the re-rating from 15x to 27x.

### 4. Price Level and Return Context versus Fundamental Path

| Period | Price | Return |
|---|---|---|
| 2024-03-27 | $49.77 | — |
| 2024-06-27 | $47.45 | –4.7% |
| 2024-09-26 | $52.85 | +11.4% |
| 2024-12-26 | $59.98 | +13.5% |
| 2025-03-31 | **$61.71** | **+2.9%** |

Annual return is **+24.0%**. The 52-week range is $44.50–$66.50; the stock is at 93% of its high. Over this same annual period, TTM EPS has declined and net margin has compressed by ~650 bps. The price rally has been entirely a multiple-expansion story—earnings have not cooperated. The most recent quarter showed deceleration in price momentum (+2.9% vs. +13.5% prior quarter), potentially reflecting early investor unease at the valuation level.

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside

**Upside:**
- Revenue trajectory is unambiguously positive: four consecutive quarters of sequential growth from the $12.70B trough, with YoY growth re-accelerating to +9.4%.
- Gross profit grew +10.9% YoY, and gross margin expanded from 64.1% to 65.1%, suggesting improving revenue quality and pricing.
- If operating expense headwinds (likely acquisition-related) are transitory and margins normalise toward the prior 23.5% net margin, earnings could recover to ~$0.85–$0.90+ EPS per quarter, which at the current price would bring the P/E back toward 18x—historically the mid-range.

**Downside:**
- The P/E of 26.9x is at the ceiling of the trailing range while earnings are declining. If the market re-rates the multiple even to the 1-year median (~20x) without earnings recovery, the implied price decline is ~25%.
- Operating income has halved from peak ($4.25B) to the current run-rate (~$2.6–$3.1B/quarter). There is no supplied evidence that this is reverting.
- Debt has tripled to $20.10B; without cash-flow data, the capacity to fund dividends, buybacks, and deleveraging cannot be confirmed.
- The stock is 93% of its 52-week high with decelerating quarterly returns, while the underlying earnings trajectory is negative.

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

CSCO presents a **negative quantitative skew** at the current juncture. The price has re-rated 77% on P/E (from 15.2x to 26.9x) while EPS has declined –6.2% YoY and net margin has compressed from 23.5% to 17.0%. The revenue recovery is real (+9.4% YoY, four sequential quarters of growth), but the earnings have not followed: operating income remains roughly half its FY23 level, and the acquisition-driven debt load ($20.10B, up from $7.70B) introduces balance-sheet risk that cannot be further evaluated without cash-flow data. At $61.71—93% of the 52-week high—the stock is priced for a margin recovery that is not yet visible in the supplied numbers. The downside from multiple compression toward the 1-year median P/E (~20x) is approximately –25%; the upside requires sustained earnings acceleration that would need to be demonstrated over multiple coming quarters. On a point-in-time, numbers-only basis, the risk/reward is unfavorable.
CSCO.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment: Cisco Systems (CSCO)**
**Decision Date: 2025-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 10-K, 2024-09-05)**
Cisco is a global technology provider focused on networking, security, collaboration, and observability. Its core strategy is to "securely connect everything" to help customers modernize infrastructure, improve cybersecurity, and harness AI and data. The company serves a broad customer base (enterprise, public sector, service providers) through a mix of direct sales and a large channel partner network. Its competitive position is built on an integrated architectural approach, a shift towards software and subscriptions, and the strategic acquisition of Splunk to enhance its security and observability data capabilities. The business is organized into three geographic segments: Americas, EMEA, and APJC.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated, from Supplied Text)**
*   **Strategic Partnerships & Market Positioning:**
    *   **2025-03-18:** Cisco is collaborating with NVIDIA, T-Mobile, and others to develop an AI-native wireless network stack for 6G, positioning it in next-generation telecom infrastructure.
    *   **2025-03-18:** Cisco is identified among companies preparing for quantum computing threats by working on post-quantum cryptography solutions.
*   **Market Tailwinds (General Industry Trends):**
    *   **2025-03-25 to 2025-03-04:** Multiple market research reports project strong growth in sectors relevant to Cisco's portfolio, including Video Surveillance-as-a-Service, hybrid cloud, industrial cybersecurity, AI-powered storage, and healthcare cybersecurity/mobility.
*   **Financial Narrative & Sentiment:**
    *   **2025-02-14 to 2025-02-12:** Web context indicates Cisco's recent results were driven by demand for AI infrastructure, leading to a raised annual outlook and an optimistic near-term forecast for its networking and security businesses.
    *   **2025-03-08:** Cisco is cited as an undervalued dividend tech stock in a market commentary.
*   **Corporate & Legal Developments:**
    *   **2025-03-10:** A report references a past whistleblower case (James Glenn) concerning alleged cybersecurity failures in Cisco's surveillance products, resolved under the False Claims Act.
    *   **2025-02-26:** A legal commentary discusses the implications of a Supreme Court cert denial in a patent case (Centripetal Networks), relevant to the broader environment of patent litigation involving large tech companies.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Intense Competition:** The 10-K (2024-09-05) details competition from numerous players (e.g., Arista, Palo Alto, Juniper, cloud giants) and notes "price-focused competition from competitors in Asia, especially from China."
    *   **Execution & Integration Risk:** Successfully integrating the large Splunk acquisition and executing on the strategy to infuse security and AI across its portfolio is a significant ongoing challenge.
    *   **Legal & Reputational Risk:** The referenced whistleblower case (2025-03-10) highlights potential reputational and legal exposure from product security issues. The patent litigation environment (2025-02-26) poses a general risk.
    *   **Technological Disruption:** The 10-K cites the risk of failing to predict and respond to emerging trends, which is critical in fast-moving areas like AI and security.
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Strategic Alignment with Key Trends:** Cisco's stated priorities (modernizing infrastructure, cybersecurity, AI/data) are directly aligned with the strong market growth trends cited in the recent news (e.g., hybrid cloud, industrial cybersecurity).
    *   **Embedded Position in Critical Infrastructure:** Its vast installed base in enterprise networking and service provider networks provides a durable foundation for cross-selling software and security.
    *   **Partnerships for Next-Gen Tech:** The collaboration with NVIDIA on 6G (2025-03-18) demonstrates an effort to co-develop and shape future technology standards.
    *   **Shift to Recurring Revenue:** The ongoing transition to software and subscriptions, as described in the 10-K, aims to improve revenue predictability.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Cautiously Positive.** The evidence points to a company strategically repositioning around high-growth areas (AI, security, cloud) with recent narrative indicating positive demand momentum. Its scale, installed base, and partnerships provide a solid foundation. However, this is balanced by significant execution risks related to its transformation, intense competition, and the need to prove the value of its integrated platform strategy against more focused rivals. The legal and reputational risks, while not currently acute, remain a background factor. The qualitative picture is of a legacy leader in transition, with its future success hinging on effective execution.

The qualitative narrative around AI infrastructure and security is supportive, but the valuation now prices in a margin recovery that is not yet visible in the numbers, leaving the risk/reward roughly balanced at current levels.

The qualitative narrative around AI infrastructure and security is supportive, but the valuation now prices in a margin recovery that is not yet visible in the numbers, leaving the risk/reward roughly balanced at current levels.

Revenue has grown sequentially for four quarters from a $12.70B trough to $13.99B, with YoY growth of +9.4% and gross margin expanding from 64.1% to 65.1%, confirming a genuine demand recovery.

Operating income remains roughly half its FY23 peak ($4.25B/quarter vs. ~$2.6–$3.1B current run-rate) and debt tripled to $20.10B post-Splunk acquisition, with no cash-flow data to confirm debt-service capacity.

Qualitative positioning is constructive—partnerships with NVIDIA on 6G (2025-03-18), raised annual outlook on AI infrastructure demand (2025-02-14), and alignment with secular growth in cybersecurity and hybrid cloud—but execution risk on the Splunk integration remains significant.

Our acquisitions can range in size and complexity, from those that enhance or complement existing products and accelerate development of features to large-scale acquisitions that result in new service offerings. Our goal is to prioritize the use of our balance sheet, through cash and debt, to complete acquisitions.
We also manage a portfolio of strategic investments in both privately held and publicly traded companies focused primarily on enterprise cloud companies, technology startups and SIs. Our investments range from early to late stage companies, including investments made concurrent with a company's initial public offering. We invest in companies that we believe are digitally transforming their industries, improving customer experiences, helping us expand our solution ecosystem or supporting other corporate initiatives, including AI. We plan to continue making these types of strategic investments as opportunities arise that we find attractive, including investments in companies representing targeted businesses, technological initiatives and geographies. Our strategy includes growing our strategic investment portfolio, in part, by reinvesting proceeds from the sales of strategic investments.
7
Table of Contents
Technology, Development and Operations
We primarily deliver our solutions as highly scalable cloud computing application and platform services on a multi-tenant technology architecture. Multi-tenancy is an architectural approach that allows us to operate a single application instance for multiple organizations, treating all customers as separate tenants who run in virtual isolation from each other. This approach allows us to spread the cost of delivering our services across our user base and scale our business faster than traditional software vendors while focusing our resources on building new functionality and enhancing existing offerings.
We provide our services through cloud computing platform partners who offer Infrastructure-as-a-Service, including servers, storage, databases and networking, as well as through infrastructure designed and operated by us but secured within third-party data center facilities. We continue to invest and expand the deployment of Hyperforce, which allows our platform and applications to be delivered rapidly and reliably to locations worldwide and provides our customers autonomy and control over data residency.
Our technology and product efforts are focused on improving and enhancing the features, functionality, performance, availability and security of our existing service offerings, as well as developing new features, functionality and services. We also remain focused on integrating businesses, services and technologies from acquisitions. Performance, functional depth, security, usability, ease of integration and configuration and sustainability of our solutions influence our technology decisions and product direction.
Competition
The market for our service offerings is highly competitive, rapidly evolving and fragmented, and subject to changing technology with low barriers to entry, shifting customer needs and frequent introductions of new products and services.
Our current competitors include:
- vendors of packaged business software, as well as companies offering enterprise applications delivered through on-premises offerings from enterprise software application vendors and cloud computing application service providers, either individually or with others;
- software companies that provide their product or service free of charge as a single product or when bundled with other offerings, or only charge a premium for advanced features and functionality, as well as companies that offer solutions that are sold without a direct sales organization;
- vendors who offer software tailored to specific services, industries or market segments, as opposed to our full suite of service offerings including suppliers of traditional business intelligence and data preparation products, integration software vendors, marketing vendors, e-commerce solutions vendors, or AI software and service vendors;
- productivity tool and email providers, unified communications providers and consumer application companies that have entered the business software market; and
- traditional platform development environment companies and cloud computing development platform companies who may develop toolsets and products that allow customers to build new applications, including AI augmented applications, that run on the customers' current infrastructure or as hosted services, as well as would-be customers who may develop enterprise applications for internal use.
We believe more companies may become competitive threats due to the attractiveness of the markets in which we operate. We also expect our competition to change and evolve as we expand into more markets, with new offerings.
Customers
We sell to businesses of all sizes and in almost every industry worldwide. The number of paying subscriptions at each of our customers ranges from one to hundreds of thousands. None of our customers accounted for more than ten percent of our revenues in fiscal years 2025, 2024 or 2023. In addition, we do not have any material dependencies on any specific product, service or particular group or groups.
Customer Service and Support
We offer professional services to help customers achieve business results faster with Salesforce solutions. Our architects and innovation program teams act as advisors to plan and execute digital transformations for our customers. This includes implementation services for multi-offering and complex deployments. We provide best-practices and AI-based recommendations and adoption programs globally. In addition, we provide advanced education, including in-person and online courses, to certify our customers and partners on architecting, administering, deploying and developing our service offerings.
Our global customer support group responds to both business and technical inquiries about the use of our products via the web, telephone, email, social networks and other channels. We provide standard customer support during regular business hours to customers as part of our paying subscription editions. We also offer premier customer support that is either included in a premium success offering or sold for an additional fee, which can include services such as priority access to technical resources, developer support and system administration. In addition, we offer a premier priority support add-on that includes proactive monitoring, rapid incident response and instruction from a dedicated support team knowledgeable about the customer's specific

CRM.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: CRM (Salesforce) as of 2025-04-01**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
- **Revenue & Earnings Growth:** TTM revenue is $38.05B. Latest Q4 (2025-01-31) revenue is $9.99B, showing QoQ growth of +5.8% and YoY growth of +7.6%. Net income of $1.71B grew QoQ by +11.9% and YoY by +18.1%. TTM net income is $6.11B.
- **Margins:** Q4 gross margin: 77.8% ($7.78B / $9.99B). Q4 operating margin: 18.2% ($1.82B / $9.99B). Q4 net margin: 17.1% ($1.71B / $9.99B). The latest net margin (0.164) shows improvement over prior quarters (e.g., 0.121 in Q3 FY2024).
- **Cash Flow:** Free cash flow data is unavailable for all periods provided.
- **Balance Sheet:** Total assets of $102.93B, total equity of $61.17B, and total debt of $8.43B. The debt-to-equity ratio is low at 0.138.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** Net income growth is strong (QoQ +11.9%, YoY +18.1%). The improvement in net margin (from 0.119 in early 2024 to 0.164) suggests enhanced profitability. ROE is 0.101.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Debt-to-equity is 0.138, indicating minimal leverage. Total equity has grown from $57.41B (Q1 FY2023) to $61.17B. Total assets increased from $93.54B to $102.93B over the same period. Cash flow data is unavailable to assess cash generation.

**3. Valuation Versus Company History**
- Valuation ratios (as of 2025-03-31) are at the low end of their 365-day historical ranges: PE diluted = 42.195 (range: 42.2 - 77.7), P/B = 4.273 (range: 3.6 - 6.1), net margin = 0.164 (range: 0.1 - 0.2).
- Over the past year, the PE has declined from 71.967 (2024-04-01) to 42.195, while net margin has expanded from 0.119 to 0.164 and ROE from 0.069 to 0.101.

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context**
- Price (2025-03-31): $268.36. Over the past year, the price range is $212.00 to $369.00.
- Quarterly return (63 trading days from 2024-12-26): -21.5%. Annual return (252 trading days from 2024-03-27): -11.0%.
- Recent declines contrast with fundamental improvement: revenue grew +7.6% YoY and earnings +19.7% YoY.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
- **Upside:** PE multiple is near the bottom of its 1-year range (42.2). If it reverted toward the midpoint (~60), the implied price increase would be approximately +42%. Improving profitability (margin expansion from 0.119 to 0.164) and low leverage support valuation resilience.
- **Downside:** Absence of cash flow data prevents assessment of cash generation quality. Primary risk is further multiple contraction if growth decelerates. A decline to the annual low of $212.00 represents -21% downside from the current price.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The stock trades at a PE (42.195) at the low end of its historical range while showing improving margins and earnings growth. The balance sheet is strong with low debt (D/E=0.138). Price has underperformed fundamentals (-11.0% annual return vs. +7.6% revenue growth). The risk is missing cash flow data and potential multiple compression. The reward is a reversion toward historical valuation norms supported by margin expansion. Quantitatively, the upside from mean reversion (~42%) outweighs the downside to the annual low (~21%), suggesting a favorable risk/reward profile based on point-in-time metrics.
CRM.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Analysis Report: Salesforce (CRM)**
**Decision Date:** 2025-04-01  
**Evidence Cutoff:** Strictly before 2025-04-01

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Salesforce is a global leader in Customer Relationship Management (CRM), providing a unified, AI-powered platform spanning sales, service, marketing, commerce, analytics, and collaboration. Its core architecture connects customer data to create a single source of truth, enabling personalized and automated interactions. A central strategic pillar is **Agentforce**, its agentic AI layer that deploys autonomous AI agents ("digital labor") to augment human teams across business functions, aiming to drive productivity and efficiency. The company operates on a scalable multi-tenant cloud architecture and sells primarily through subscriptions to businesses of all sizes globally, supported by a vast partner ecosystem (AppExchange) and a developer community.

*(Source: 10-K Business Section, 2025-03-05)*

### 2. Recent Narrative Developments (Dated)
*   **Financial Outlook & Guidance (2025-02-26):** For fiscal year 2026 (ending Jan 2026), Salesforce projects revenue growth of **7-8%** to a range of $40.5-$40.9 billion, with a targeted non-GAAP operating margin of **34%**. The commentary emphasizes "cautious" guidance and prudent investment for scalable profitability.
    *(Source: Web context, earnings call transcript summary)*
*   **AI Strategy & Product Momentum (2025-03-14, 2025-03-13):** CEO Marc Benioff is actively positioning **Agentforce** as the catalyst for a "digital labor revolution," citing strong customer adoption. The platform is highlighted for its unified AI architecture and high accuracy rates in automating workflows.
    *(Source: Company news articles)*
*   **Partnerships & Use Cases (2025-03-12):** Singapore Airlines partnered with Salesforce to develop AI-powered customer service solutions, indicating expansion in the aviation industry vertical.
    *(Source: Company news)*
*   **Market Position & Analyst Sentiment (2025-03-17, 2025-03-18):** Salesforce is repeatedly cited in financial press as a quality long-term tech holding and an "early AI opportunity." Analyst consensus from March 2025 implies significant upside, though risks of target revisions are noted.
    *(Source: Company news and web context)*
*   **Legal Disposition (2025-03-31):** A federal judge dismissed a patent infringement lawsuit against Salesforce concerning database software, resolving a legal matter in the company's favor.
    *(Source: Risk web context)*

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Competitive & Market Fragmentation:** The 10-K explicitly states the market is "highly competitive, rapidly evolving and fragmented" with "low barriers to entry." Competition comes from packaged software, free-tier providers, niche vendors, and platform developers.
    *(Source: 10-K Competition Section, 2025-03-05)*
*   **Execution & Adoption Risk (AI):** The success of the growth strategy hinges on driving adoption of new products like Agentforce and Data Cloud. There is a risk that customer uptake may not meet expectations or that the technology may not deliver the projected productivity gains.
    *(Inference from Strategy sections of 10-K and product emphasis in news)*
*   **Operational & Security Risks:** The company's risk disclosures include maintaining security, preventing downtime, and data protection. A breach or significant service disruption could damage trust, its stated core value.
    *(Source: Risk context from investor filing, 2025-02-26)*
*   **Regulatory & Compliance Risk:** The 10-K grounds its values in legal and regulatory frameworks. Evidence from Japan (2025-02-19) indicates historical challenges with local disability employment quotas, highlighting potential compliance gaps in international operations.
    *(Source: 10-K values text; Wikipedia risk snippet)*

**Offsets:**
*   **Deep, Unified Platform & Ecosystem:** The breadth of its integrated platform and the developer/partner ecosystem (AppExchange) create high switching costs and cross-selling opportunities, which are core to its growth strategy.
    *(Source: 10-K Business & Strategy Sections)*
*   **Recurring Revenue & Customer Base:** A subscription model with a broad, diversified customer base (no single client >10% of revenue) provides revenue visibility and reduces concentration risk.
    *(Source: 10-K Customers Section)*
*   **Proactive AI Investment & Positioning:** Salesforce is not a passive participant in the AI shift; it is aggressively building and commercializing Agentforce, positioning it as a central platform for enterprise AI agents, which could strengthen its competitive moat if successful.
    *(Source: Multiple recent news articles and 10-K strategy)*

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Positive.**

The franchise is demonstrably strong as the global CRM leader with a deeply integrated platform, creating significant customer lock-in. Recent narrative is dominated by a coherent and well-funded strategic push into agentic AI (Agentforce), which addresses a key enterprise demand. While growth guidance is moderate (7-8%), the focus on profitable growth (34% margin) is a positive signal. Key risks are inherent in the highly competitive software market and the execution risk of a major product cycle, but these are partially offset by the company's scale, ecosystem, and proactive strategy. The qualitative evidence supports a view of a durable business with a relevant, forward-looking catalyst.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Salesforce trades at a PE of 42.2, near the bottom of its 1-year range (42.2–77.7), while delivering improving profitability (net margin expanded from 0.119 to 0.164) and strong earnings growth (+18.1% YoY). The combination of a durable CRM franchise, a coherent AI strategy via Agentforce, and a low-leverage balance sheet (D/E=0.138) supports a favorable risk/reward profile with ~42% upside to historical mean valuation versus ~21% downside to the annual low.

ITEM 1.    BUSINESS
Introduction
The Sherwin-Williams Company, founded in 1866 and incorporated in Ohio in 1884, is engaged in the development, manufacture, distribution and sale of paint, coatings and related products to professional, industrial, commercial and retail customers primarily in North and South America with additional operations in the Caribbean region, Europe, Asia and Australia. Our principal executive offices are located at 101 West Prospect Avenue, Cleveland, Ohio 44115-1075, telephone (216) 566-2000. As used in this report, the terms "Sherwin-Williams," "Company," "we", "us" and "our" mean The Sherwin-Williams Company and its consolidated subsidiaries.
Available Information
We make available free of charge on or through our website our Annual Reports on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, Current Reports on Form 8-K and amendments to these reports, as soon as reasonably practicable after we electronically file such material with, or furnish such material to, the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). You may access these documents on our Investor Relations website, investors.sherwin.com.
We also make available free of charge on our website our Corporate Governance Guidelines, our Director Independence Standards, our Code of Conduct and the charters of our Audit Committee, our Compensation and Management Development Committee and our Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee. You may access these documents on our Investor Relations website, investors.sherwin.com.
Basis of Reportable Segments
The Company reports its segment information in the same way that management internally organizes its business for assessing performance and making decisions regarding allocation of resources. The Company has three reportable operating segments: Paint Stores Group, Consumer Brands Group and Performance Coatings Group (individually, a Reportable Segment and collectively, the Reportable Segments). The Company reports all other business activities and immaterial operating segments that are not reportable in the Administrative function. For more information about the Reportable Segments, see Note 22 to the consolidated financial statements in Item 8.
Paint Stores Group
Paint Stores Group consisted of 4,773 company-operated specialty paint stores in the United States, Canada and the Caribbean region at December 31, 2024. Each store is engaged in servicing the needs of architectural and industrial paint contractors and do-it-yourself homeowners. These stores market and sell Sherwin-Williams® and other controlled brand architectural paint and coatings, protective and marine products, OEM product finishes and related products. The majority of these products are produced by manufacturing facilities in the Consumer Brands Group. In addition, each store sells select purchased associated products. The loss of any single customer would not have a material adverse effect on the business of this segment.
Consumer Brands Group
The Consumer Brands Group manufactures and distributes a broad portfolio of branded and private-label architectural paint, stains, varnishes, industrial products, wood finishes products, wood preservatives, applicators, corrosion inhibitors, aerosols, caulks and adhesives to retailers, including home centers an
…[truncated 13771 chars]…
unds and experiences. During 2024, we hired approximately 1,500 professionals through our management trainee program as part of our long-term growth initiatives.
We invest in our people by providing learning and employee networking opportunities to drive retention, development and engagement and help employees excel in their current and future roles. During 2024, our employees collectively completed thousands of hours of online and instructor-led courses across a broad range of categories, including leadership, professional skills, technical skills and compliance. We measure our progress toward creating a culture of excellence that empowers employees to learn, grow and achieve their aspirations by conducting periodic pulse surveys and a global engagement survey, which we conducted in 2023 and expect to conduct every other year. We are focused on using these survey results to drive continued progress with our efforts.
Occupational Health and Safety.
Providing safe and healthy working environments for our employees is a core value. We have a consistent focus on Environmental, Health and Safety excellence that promotes employee health and safety, process safety and occupational health, including evaluation and implementation of preventative measures to reduce workplace injuries and illness. We strive for incident-free workplaces and are continuously seeking to improve the programs that are in place to help keep our employees, customers and communities safe, including by regularly re-evaluating our global management systems, standards and performance measures.
Total Rewards.
We prioritize the fair and consistent treatment of our employees in relation to working conditions, wages, benefits, policies and procedures. The Company's policies and programs are designed to respond to the needs of our employees in a manner that provides a safe, professional, efficient and rewarding workplace. Our total rewards programs are designed to offer competitive compensation, comprehensive benefits and other programs to support employees' growth, both personally and professionally, and the needs and well-being of our employees worldwide.
Over the past few years, we have enhanced certain of the Company's benefits and practices to support the health and well-being of our employees. Our enhanced benefits have included paid sick leave, family leave and voluntary leave of absence policies and programs. In 2024, we introduced a new education benefit that offers bachelors' degrees, associates' degrees and certificates for in-demand fields, with tuition fully paid by our Company. More recently, in 2025 we added a new backup child and elder care benefit. We continue to permit remote, alternate and flexible work arrangements where possible to promote increased flexibility and support employee health and well-being, while maintaining our focus on collaboration and engagement.
Regulatory Compliance
For additional information regarding environmental-related matters, see Notes 1, 10 and 19 to the consolidated financial statements in Item 8.
4
Table of Contents
CAUTIONARY STATEMENT REGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION
Certain statements contained in "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations" and elsewhere in this report constitute "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of federal securities laws. These forward-looking statements are based upon management's current expectations, predictions, estimates, assumptions and beliefs concerning future events and conditions and may discuss, among other things, anticipated future performance (including sales and earnings), expected growth, future business plans and the costs and potential liability for environmental-related matters and lead pigment and lead-based paint litigation. Any statement that is not historical in nature is a forward-looking statement and may be identified by the use of words and phrases such as "anticipate," "aspire," "believe," "could," "estimate," "expect," "goal," "intend," "may," "plan," "potential," "project," "seek," "should," "strive," "target," "will," or "would" or the negative thereof or comparable terminology.
Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on any forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements are necessarily subject to risks, uncertainties and other factors, many of which are outside our control, that could cause actual results to differ materially from such statements and from our historical results, performance and experience. These risks, uncertainties and other factors include such things as:
- general business and economic conditions in the United States and worldwide;
- inflation rates, interest rates, unemployment rates, labor costs, healthcare costs, recessionary conditions, geopolitical conditions, terrorist activity, armed conflicts and wars, public health crises, pandemics, outbreaks of disease and supply chain disruptions;
- shifts in consumer behavior driven by economic downturns in cyclical segments of the economy;
- shortages and increases in the cost of raw materials and energy;
- catastrophic events, adverse weather conditions and natural disasters (including those that may be related to climate change);
- the loss of any of our largest customers;
- increased competition or failure to keep pace with developments in key competitive areas of our business;
- disruptions to our information technology systems, including due to digitization efforts or cybersecurity incidents;
- our ability to attract, retain, develop and progress a qualified global workforce;
- our ability to successfully integrate past and future acquisitions into our existing operations;
- risks and uncertainties associated with our expansion into and our operations in South America, Asia, Europe and other foreign markets;
- policy changes affecting international trade, including import/export restrictions and tariffs;
- our ability to achieve our strategies or expectations relating to sustainability considerations, including as a result of evolving legal, regulatory and other standards, processes and assumptions, the pace of scientific and technological developments, increased costs, the availability of requisite suppliers, energy sources, or financing and changes in carbon markets;
- damage to our business, reputation, image or brands due to negative publicity;
- the infringement or loss of our intellectual property rights or the theft or unauthorized use of our trade secrets or other confidential business information;
- a weakening of global credit markets or changes to our credit ratings;
- our ability to generate cash to service our indebtedness;
- fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates and changing monetary policies;
- our ability to comply with a variety of complex U.S. and non-U.S. laws, rules and regulations;
- increases in tax rates, or changes in tax laws or regulations;
- our ability to comply with numerous, complex and increasingly stringent domestic and foreign health, safety and environmental laws, regulations and requirements;
- our liability related to environmental investigation and remediation activities at some of our currently- and formerly-owned sites;
- the nature, cost, quantity and outcome of pending and future litigation, including lead pigment and lead-based paint litigation; and
- the other risk factors discussed in Item 1A of this Annual Report on Form 10-K and our other reports filed with the SEC.

SHW.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Equity Analysis: SHW**
**Date: 2025-04-01 | Evidence cutoff: 2025-03-31**

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory
*   **Revenue:** TTM revenue of $23.10B. Quarterly revenue shows high volatility, ranging from $5.25B (Q4 2023) to $6.27B (Q2 2024). The latest quarter (Q4 2024) reported $5.30B, a QoQ decline of -14.0% but a YoY increase of +0.9%.
*   **Earnings:** TTM net income of $2.68B. Quarterly EPS fluctuates significantly ($1.40 to $3.50). The latest quarter (Q4 2024) reported EPS of $1.90, a QoQ decline of -40.3% but a YoY increase of +35.7%.
*   **Margins:** TTM gross margin of 48.5%, TTM operating margin of 14.9%, TTM net margin of 11.6%. The latest quarter (Q4 2024) reported an operating loss of -$166.60M, despite positive net income of $480.10M.
*   **Cash-Flow:** Data is entirely unavailable.
*   **Balance-Sheet Trajectory:** Total equity has grown from $3.17B (Q1 2023) to $4.05B (Q4 2024). Total debt has remained high, ranging from $8.98B to $9.60B. Total assets are approximately stable around $23.6B.

### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience
*   **Earnings Quality:** A major discrepancy exists in the latest quarter (Q4 2024). An operating loss of -$166.60M coexists with positive net income of $480.10M. This suggests earnings may be significantly boosted by non-operating items (e.g., tax benefits, asset sales, or investment gains). The high TTM net margin of 11.6% is inconsistent with the TTM operating margin of 14.9% only if the quarterly pattern of operating losses offset by non-operating gains is consistent. The volatility in quarterly earnings raises questions about durability.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The balance sheet shows a persistent high leverage profile. As of Q4 2024: Total Debt = $9.23B, Total Equity = $4.05B, resulting in a Debt/Equity ratio of 2.277x. Total liabilities are $19.58B against total assets of $23.63B. The equity base is thin relative to the asset base and debt load.

### 3. Valuation Versus Supplied History
*   Current valuation multiples (as of 2025-03-31): P/E (diluted) = 33.10, P/B = 21.90, P/S = 3.84.
*   Over the past year (2024-04-01 to 2025-03-31), the P/E ranged from 30.9 to 39.7, and the P/B ranged from 20.2 to 26.4. The current P/E of 33.10 is at the lower end of this range, while the P/B of 21.90 is near the midpoint.
*   The net margin has expanded from 0.104 (2024-04-01) to 0.116 (2025-03-31), and ROE from 0.643 to 0.662. The current valuation, on a P/E basis, is modestly cheaper relative to its recent history, but this is against a backdrop of questionable earnings quality and high leverage.

### 4. Price Level and Return Context Versus Fundamentals
*   **Price Level:** The stock closed at $349.19 on 2025-03-31, well below its 52-week high of $400.42.
*   **Recent Returns:** Quarterly return (last ~63 days) = +1.1%. Annual return (last ~252 days) = +0.7%. The stock has underperformed over the annual period.
*   **Path vs. Fundamentals:** The stock price showed a sharp +29.3% rally from June to September 2024, followed by a -10.0% decline from September to December 2024. This volatility in price does not align with the relatively stable, if volatile, quarterly revenue trends. The weak annual return (+0.7%) coincides with periods of high earnings volatility and a high-leverage balance sheet.

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside
*   **Strongest Upside:** The company's TTM ROE is extremely high at 66.2% (0.662), indicating highly efficient use of equity capital. This is the primary fundamental strength. Additionally, the gross margin is robust at 48.5%.
*   **Strongest Downside:** The critical risk is the disconnect between operating and net income in the most recent quarter, questioning earnings durability. The balance sheet carries significant leverage (Debt/Equity = 2.277x). The stock's valuation, while at the lower end of its recent P/E range, remains premium (P/E=33.10, P/B=21.90) and offers a minimal earnings yield of 0.03 (3.0%).

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion
The quantitative profile presents a conflict. **Positive** factors are a strong TTM ROE (0.662), high gross margin (0.485), and a P/E ratio near the bottom of its recent range (33.1 vs. 30.9-39.7). **Negative** factors are severe: the Q4 2024 operating loss (-$166.60M) calls into question the quality of reported net income ($480.10M), the balance sheet is highly leveraged (Debt/Equity = 2.277), and the stock's annual return is flat (+0.7%) despite premium valuations. The absence of cash flow data precludes a full assessment. The valuation does not appear compensatory for the evident risks in earnings quality and balance sheet resilience. The risk/reward is unfavorable based on the supplied point-in-time numerical evidence.
SHW.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Research Report - The Sherwin-Williams Company (SHW)**
**Assessment Date: 2025-04-01**
**Evidence Cutoff: 2025-03-25**

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Based on the 10-K business description (filed 2025-02-20, period end 2024-12-31), Sherwin-Williams operates as a global manufacturer and distributor of paints and coatings. The business is structured into three core segments: **Paint Stores Group** (4,773 company-operated stores in the U.S., Canada, and Caribbean, serving contractors and DIY customers), **Consumer Brands Group** (manufactures and distributes branded/private-label products to retailers, includes 334 stores in Latin America), and **Performance Coatings Group** (industrial coatings for wood, automotive, marine, and industrial applications worldwide). The company emphasizes strong brand recognition, a broad integrated network from manufacturing to retail distribution, and competition based on product quality, innovation, service, and pricing.

### 2. Recent Developments (Supplied Evidence, Dated)
*   **Strategic Expansion (2025-02-18):** Sherwin-Williams agreed to acquire BASF's Brazilian architectural paints division, Suvinil, for $1.15 billion. This is stated to strengthen its presence in the key Brazilian market.
*   **Favorable Market Trends (2025-02-18 & 2025-02-25):** External market reports project growth in related coating segments. The anti-corrosion coating market is expected to reach $54.4 billion by 2033 (CAGR 5.4%), driven by infrastructure and sustainable coatings (2025-02-18). The UV-curable coatings market is projected to reach $20.94 billion by 2032, driven by demand in automotive, electronics, and packaging (2025-02-25).
*   **Analyst Sentiment (2025-02-23):** A financial commentary noted a mean analyst price target of $397.32 for SHW, representing an 11.8% premium to prevailing levels at the time, with a Street-high target suggesting ~24.9% upside. The stock was also noted for a 5.1% gain adding to one-year returns (2025-03-02).
*   **Industry Engagement (2025-03-03):** Sherwin-Williams is listed as a participant in a specialty chemicals symposium focusing on topics like pricing power and margin recovery.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Macroeconomic & Cyclical Sensitivity (Confirmed - from 10-K):** The company's performance is tied to construction, industrial activity, and consumer spending, making it vulnerable to recessions, inflation, interest rates, and geopolitical disruptions.
*   **Raw Material Cost Volatility (Confirmed - from 10-K):** Key raw materials are derived from petrochemical feedstocks. Price fluctuations and availability pose a risk to cost of goods sold.
*   **Integration & Expansion Risk (Inferred from Acquisition News):** The $1.15 billion acquisition of Suvinil in Brazil (2025-02-18) represents a significant strategic expansion into an emerging market, carrying inherent execution and integration risks.
*   **Intense Competition (Confirmed - from 10-K):** The company faces competition from local to international players across all segments, necessitating ongoing investment in innovation and marketing.

**Offsets:**
*   **Integrated Business Model (Confirmed - from 10-K):** The ownership of manufacturing and ~5,100+ company-operated stores provides supply chain control, direct customer access, and distribution advantages.
*   **Mitigation Strategies (Confirmed - from 10-K):** The company states it uses inventory management, strategic supplier relationships, and alternative sourcing to mitigate raw material risks.
*   **Market Positioning (Inferred from Market Reports & News):** The company's portfolio is aligned with growth trends in performance, protection, and sustainable coatings, potentially offsetting cyclical pressures in some segments.

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Positive.**
The evidence confirms a durable franchise with a significant, integrated physical and brand presence. Recent developments are dominated by a material strategic acquisition to expand into a large emerging market (Brazil, 2025-02-18), positioning the company for long-term growth. This is complemented by external analyses pointing to favorable tailwinds in key performance coating categories. While the 10-K outlines standard industry risks, the point-in-time narrative highlights a proactive strategic move to enhance scale and geographic diversification.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Sherwin-Williams presents a mixed picture: a durable franchise with strong brand and integrated distribution (4,773+ stores) offset by questionable earnings quality (Q4 2024 operating loss of -$166.6M vs. net income of $480.1M), high leverage (Debt/Equity 2.277x), and premium valuation (P/E 33.1, P/B 21.9) that doesn't adequately compensate for balance-sheet and earnings-durability risks.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Sherwin-Williams presents a mixed picture: a durable franchise with strong brand and integrated distribution (4,773+ stores) offset by questionable earnings quality (Q4 2024 operating loss of -$166.6M vs. net income of $480.1M), high leverage (Debt/Equity 2.277x), and premium valuation (P/E 33.1, P/B 21.9) that doesn't adequately compensate for balance-sheet and earnings-durability risks.

We also enable interactive graphics applications - such as games, movie and photo editing and design software - to be accessed by almost any device, almost anywhere, through our cloud platforms such as vGPU for enterprise and GeForce NOW for gaming.
Advancing the leading autonomous vehicle platform.
We believe the advent of autonomous vehicles, or AV, and electric vehicles, or EV, is revolutionizing the transportation industry. The algorithms required for autonomous driving - such as perception, localization, and planning - are too complex for legacy hand-coded approaches and will use multiple neural networks instead. Therefore, we provide an AI-based hardware and software solution, designed and implemented from the ground up based on automotive safety standards, for the AV and EV market under the DRIVE brand, which we are bringing to market through our partnerships with automotive OEMs, tier-1 suppliers, and start-ups. Our AV solution also includes the GPU-based hardware required to train the neural networks before their in-vehicle deployment, as well as to re-simulate their operation prior to any over-the-air software updates. We believe our comprehensive, top-to-bottom and end-to-end approach will enable the transportation industry to solve the complex problems arising from the shift to autonomous driving.
Leveraging our intellectual property, or IP.
We believe our IP is a valuable asset that can be accessed by our customers and partners through license and development agreements when they desire to build such capabilities directly into their own products or have us do so through a custom development. Such license and development arrangements can further enhance the reach of our technology.
Sales and Marketing
Our worldwide sales and marketing strategy is key to achieving our objective of providing markets with our high-performance and efficient computing platforms and software. Our sales and marketing teams, located across our global markets, work closely with customers and various industry ecosystems through our partner network. Our partner network incorporates global, regional and specialized CSPs, OEMs, ODMs, ISVs, global system integrators, add-in board manufacturers, or AIBs, distributors, automotive manufacturers and tier-1 automotive suppliers, and other ecosystem participants.
Members of our sales team have technical expertise and product and industry knowledge. We also employ a team of application engineers and solution architects to provide pre-sales assistance to our partner network in designing, testing, and qualifying system designs that incorporate our platforms. We believe that the depth and quality of our design support are key to improving our partner network's time-to-market, maintaining a high level of customer satisfaction, and fostering relationships that encourage our customers and partner network to use the next generation of our products within each platform.
